lede: A look at January 27 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Park Seong-hoon (footballer) and Chanda Gunn, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Arab Spring
events.desc: Arab Spring: The Yemeni Revolution begins as over 16,000 protestors demonstrate in Sanaa.

events.title: National Recording Registry
events.desc: The first selections for the National Recording Registry are announced by the Library of Congress.

events.title: Iran hostage crisis
events.desc: Through cooperation between the U.S. and Canadian governments, six American diplomats secretly escape hostilities in Iran in the culmination of the Canadian Caper.

events.title: Trần Văn Hương
events.desc: South Vietnamese Prime Minister Trần Văn Hương is removed by the military junta of Nguyễn Khánh.

events.title: Bundaberg tragedy
events.desc: Bundaberg tragedy: a diphtheria vaccine is contaminated with Staph. aureus bacterium, resulting in the deaths of twelve children in the Australian town of Bundaberg.

events.title: University of Georgia
events.desc: The University of Georgia is founded, the first state-chartered public university in the United States.

events.title: Johann Sebastian Bach
events.desc: J. S. Bach leads the first performance of Alles nur nach Gottes Willen, BWV 72, concluding his third Christmas season in Leipzig on the Third Sunday after Epiphany.

events.title: Pope Clement VI
events.desc: Pope Clement VI issues the papal bull Unigenitus, laying out the scriptural justification for indulgences, identifying only the Pope and episcopate as capable of accessing the treasury of merit, and establishing a jubilee year every half century.

events.title: Stephen Lekapenos
events.desc: The co-emperors Stephen and Constantine are overthrown and forced to become monks by Constantine VII, who becomes sole emperor of the Byzantine Empire.

events.title: AD 98
events.desc: Trajan succeeds his adoptive father Nerva as Roman emperor.

births.name: Chanda Gunn
births.line: American ice hockey player and coach

births.name: Hans Modrow
births.line: Polish-German lawyer and politician, 5th Prime Minister of East Germany (died 2023)

births.name: Hyman G. Rickover
births.line: American admiral, leader in the development of nuclear propulsion in the US Navy (died 1986)

births.name: Eugène Viollet-le-Duc
births.line: French architect, designed the Lausanne Cathedral (died 1879)

deaths.text: Howard Zinn, American historian, author, and activist
Trần Văn Hương, South Vietnamese politician, 3rd President of South Vietnam, 3rd Vice President of South Vietnam, and 3rd Prime Minister of South Vietnam
Rocco D'Assunta, Italian actor, comedian and playwright
Johann Gottlieb Fichte, German philosopher and academic

observances.text: Christian feast day: Blessed Paul Joseph Nardini
Christian feast day: Enrique de Ossó y Cercelló
Christian feast day: John Chrysostom (translation of relics) (Anglican, Lutheran, Eastern Orthodox)
Day of the lifting of the siege of Leningrad (Russia)
